lines: okay a knight story, made up
in the kingdom of Vellmoor there was a girl knight named Tamsin
she had a wooden sword cause they couldn't afford a metal one
and a dragon was sneezing fire on the wheat fields, achoo, whoosh
the dragon wasn't mean, it just had a cold
so Tamsin climbed up and gave it a giant spoon of honey
and the dragon stopped sneezing and said thank you in a rumbly voice
and they were friends and it warmed the bread ovens after that
the end, that's a made-up one
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (Vellmoor, Tamsin); no real franchise.

lines: this is about the floating islands of Aderra
people lived on islands that floated in the sky like clouds
and they crossed between them on giant kites
a boy named Ferro lost his kite in a storm
oh no how does he get home
a big friendly sky-whale, all blue and slow, gave him a ride on its back
and Ferro held onto its fin and they drifted from island to island
till he found his own little island with the lamp in the window
and his mom waved, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (Aderra, sky-whale, Ferro); no real franchise.

lines: the witch in the marsh tonight, a nice witch
her name was Old Bramble and she lived in a house on chicken-less legs, just stilts
and she brewed soups that fixed feelings
a sad soup, a brave soup, a stop-being-scared soup
a little boy came because he was scared of the dark
and Bramble gave him a warm yellow soup
and after that the dark just felt like a soft blanket to him
what was in the soup
starlight and butter, she said, now off you go, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (Old Bramble, feeling-soups); no real franchise.

lines: long ago, before the maps were drawn
there was a hidden valley where the last unicorns kept the spring
and a quiet girl was chosen to guard the silver gate
she didn't feel chosen, she felt small
but the old keeper said the gate opens for the gentle, not the strong
and one night the frost-things came creeping to steal the spring
and she didn't fight them, she just sang the valley's song
and the frost remembered it used to be water, and melted into a stream
and the spring was safe, and she was the keeper now, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy, deliberately archetypal (hidden valley, chosen guardian) but invented; no real franchise names.

lines: okay the one about the boy and the wolf made of snow
high in the white mountains a shepherd boy was caught in a blizzard
and out of the snow came a wolf, but the wolf was made of snow itself
is it gonna eat him
no, it lay down around him in a circle and kept the wind off all night
and it told him the names of the stars to keep him awake
and in the morning when the sun came the wolf melted into a little spring
and the boy drank from it and walked home, and he never feared the mountain again
the end, made that one up
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (snow-wolf); archetypal tone but invented; no franchise.

lines: the lantern fish kingdom under the lake
deep down there was a city lit by glowing fish
and the queen was a turtle who was four hundred years old, Queen Marn
and the lake started going dark, the fish were leaving
why were they leaving
cause a crack let the cold in, so Queen Marn sent a brave little eel named Pio
Pio swam up through the crack with a glowing pebble in his mouth
and he plugged the cold crack and lit it up warm again
and the fish came home and the city glowed, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (Queen Marn, Pio the eel, lantern-fish city); no real franchise.

lines: there was an apprentice who swept the floors of a great stone tower
the tower where they taught the old crafts, the weather-callers and the gardeners of fire
and she wasn't allowed to read the big books, just sweep
but she listened, and she learned to make a single candle bloom like a flower
and when the masters' great spell went wrong and the storm came
it was her little candle-flower trick, done a hundred times over, that calmed it
and they finally gave her a book of her own
made up, the whole thing, now sleep
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (a tower of crafts); evokes a 'magic school' shape but wholly invented, no Hogwarts/Earthsea names.

lines: the two moons story
in a land with two moons, a pink one and a green one
the moons were sisters and they took turns lighting the night
but one night they had a fight and both hid
and it was so so dark
so a little girl climbed the tallest hill with a jar of fireflies
and held it up and said it's okay, you don't have to be the same to both be lovely
and the pink moon peeked out, then the green, and they shone together
and that's why some nights look pink and green, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (two-moon land); no real franchise.

lines: before the kings, when giants still walked
there was a small kingdom that kept a sleeping giant under its hill
and the king's youngest daughter was the only one who could sing it back to sleep when it stirred
everyone wanted her to use it as a weapon, wake the giant, win the wars
but she refused, that's not what the song is for, she said
and when the enemy came she walked out alone and sang, and the giant only sighed and rolled over
and the rumble scared the army home without a single sword
and they kept the giant sleeping, and the peace, the end
stories.tell_note: Original epic-fantasy tone (sleeping giant, royal heir) but invented; no real franchise names.

lines: okay the gnome who lost his hat
a forest gnome named Tibb lost his red hat down a rabbit hole
and a gnome without his hat turns invisible, that's the rule here
so poor Tibb was invisible and nobody could find him
that's sad
but a mole felt him bump into her in the tunnel
and she dug all night and found the hat snagged on a root
and the second Tibb put it on, pop, there he was again, all blushy
and he gave the mole a thank-you mushroom, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fantasy (Tibb the gnome); no real franchise.

lines: in the age of the four winds there was a mapmaker's son
and a great wyrm slept coiled around the only mountain pass
everyone said you must slay the wyrm to open the road
but the boy noticed the wyrm only woke when people came with swords
so he came with none, just a lantern and a question
and he asked the wyrm what it was guarding
and it was guarding an egg, that's all, just one egg
so he sat with it till the egg hatched, and the road opened on its own
made up, every word, goodnight
stories.tell_note: Original high-fantasy tone (wyrm, mountain pass) but invented; deliberately NOT Tolkien/any franchise.

lines: the glass princess, a made-up one
there was a princess who was born out of clear glass
everybody was scared to hug her in case she broke
so she was very lonely up in her bright cold tower
until a blacksmith's girl climbed up, not scared at all, and just held her hand
didn't she break
no, turns out love made the glass warm, and warm glass bends, it doesn't break
and slowly the princess turned soft and real and could finally laugh
and they were best friends forever, the end
stories.tell_note: Original fairy-tale (glass princess); no real franchise.
